sources.source_name: ODSC Medium - Emerging AI Jobs for 2026

example_posting.title: Context Engineer
example_posting.snippet: We are seeking a Context Engineer to design and optimize how large language models utilize their context windows. You will work on novel techniques for efficient token usage, context compression, and strategic information placement to maximize model utility and cost-effectiveness.

core_responsibilities: Design and optimize context window strategies for LLMs
Develop techniques for efficient token usage and context compression
Evaluate and benchmark context approaches against baselines
Collaborate with alignment teams on context robustness
Document best practices and create tools for internal teams
typical_background: ML engineering or research background
Strong Python and data manipulation skills
Experience with LLM APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Hugging Face)
Familiarity with prompt engineering and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG)
Understanding of transformer architectures and tokenization
notes: Often conflated with Prompt Engineer but represents a distinct specialization. Context Engineers focus on systemic/structural optimization of context usage, while Prompt Engineers craft individual prompts. Role emerged 2025 as context windows grew from 4k to 200k+ tokens. Growing fastest in frontier AI labs and enterprise AI applications.
pipeline_readiness_notes: Strong signal across multiple companies. Comparable to emerging ML roles; ready for task statement enrichment and scoring. Recommend assigning Job Zone 4.

sources.source_name: Hashnode - 2026 Guide to Forward Deployed Engineer

example_posting.title: Forward Deploy Engineer
example_posting.snippet: Forward Deploy Engineers embed with our most important customers to deploy AI systems at scale. You will architect solutions, troubleshoot production issues, develop integration patterns, and gather customer insights that inform product development. This role bridges customer needs and engineering.

core_responsibilities: Deploy and manage AI/ML systems at customer sites
Troubleshoot production issues and optimize performance
Develop custom integrations and solutions for enterprise customers
Gather product feedback and communicate customer needs to engineering
Write technical documentation and knowledge base articles
Mentor and support customer teams on AI best practices
typical_background: Software engineering or MLOps background
Experience deploying production ML systems
Strong communication and customer-facing skills
Knowledge of cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure)
Familiarity with containerization and deployment pipelines
Understanding of RAG, fine-tuning, vector databases, and agentic orchestration
notes: Hybrid technical and customer-facing role that emerged 2026. Similar to Solutions Architect but focused on AI/ML stacks. 300% FDE job growth since 2024 reported. Average total compensation now $238,000 (range $205K-$486K), with staff-level FDEs clearing $630K+. Could multiply 5x by 2028 per analyst forecasts.
pipeline_readiness_notes: Strong multi-company adoption validates role legitimacy. Ready for pipeline. May map to Solutions Architect family in O*NET but with AI-specific variants. Job Zone 4-5.

companies_hiring: Institute for AI Policy and Strategy

sources.source_name: EA Forum - AI Safety Talent Needs 2026

example_posting.title: AI Safety Researcher
example_posting.snippet: We are hiring AI Safety Researchers to conduct technical research and advocacy aimed at mitigating societal-scale risks from AI. You'll address AI's toughest challenges through technical research, field-building initiatives, and policy engagement.

core_responsibilities: Conduct technical AI safety research
Develop safety methodologies and testing frameworks
Collaborate with policy and field-building teams
Mentor junior researchers and supervise research teams
Publish findings and contribute to academic discourse
Advise on safety implications of AI development
typical_background: PhD in computer science, mathematics, or related field (preferred)
Research experience in machine learning or AI
Strong publication record in relevant fields
Understanding of AI alignment and safety concepts
Policy or governance familiarity (beneficial)
notes: Capacity-constrained by lack of senior researchers to mentor junior talent, driving hyper-selective hiring and blocking promising candidates. Significant talent influx from academic and industry backgrounds. Government investment in AI safety roles increasing. Field-building organizations report AI automation exacerbating talent bottlenecks.
pipeline_readiness_notes: Strong research and policy signals but concentrated in specialized research organizations. Monitor for broader adoption across industry. May require unique Job Zone assignment (5-6 for advanced research positions). Recommend quarterly review for expansion into industry roles.

sources.source_name: Eleken Blog - How to Become AI PM 2026
Product School - AI Product Manager Guide

example_posting.title: AI Product Manager
example_posting.snippet: As an AI Product Manager, you will drive the development of AI-native products, balancing model capabilities and limitations. You will work cross-functionally with ML researchers, engineers, and designers to bring innovative AI experiences to market.

core_responsibilities: Define product strategy for AI-native or AI-integrated products
Balance model limitations with user needs and business objectives
Work with ML teams to understand model constraints and capabilities
Define metrics for AI product success (beyond traditional PM metrics)
Manage rapid iteration cycles and model improvements
Communicate technical AI concepts to stakeholders
typical_background: Prior product management experience (preferred)
Understanding of ML/AI concepts and limitations
Ability to read and interpret ML research
Data analysis and metrics design skills
Technical communication skills
Experience with rapid experimentation
notes: PM hiring doubled in 2025 with 12,000+ new roles. As of Oct 2023, 14,000+ job openings globally (6,900 in US). Salary range $192K-$437K, median $198K in 2026. Senior AI PMs at top companies earn $286K-$569K. Distinct from traditional PM due to model dependencies, limitations, and iteration cycles.
pipeline_readiness_notes: Strong adoption across major tech companies and enterprises. Large posting volume validates market demand. Ready for enrichment and scoring. Recommend Job Zone 4-5 depending on seniority level.

sources.source_name: Oreate AI Blog - Rise of Agent Architect

example_posting.title: Agentic AI Solutions Architect
example_posting.snippet: Lead the design and delivery of Agentic AI-driven, cloud-native solutions using Python and AWS. This role requires deep expertise in agentic orchestration frameworks (LangGraph, AutoGen) and ability to architect autonomous systems that integrate with business processes.

core_responsibilities: Design complex autonomous AI systems and multi-step workflows
Define AI agent architectures for business process automation
Build agentic orchestration using frameworks like LangGraph, AutoGen
Design agent memory systems and retrieval mechanisms
Develop evaluation frameworks to ensure agent safety and correctness
Translate business requirements into quantifiable logic chains
typical_background: Software architecture or senior engineering background
Deep understanding of AI agents and autonomous systems
Proficiency with agentic orchestration frameworks
Python and cloud platform expertise (AWS, GCP, Azure)
Experience designing and deploying production AI systems
Understanding of multi-step reasoning and planning
notes: Emerging from evolution of Prompt Engineer role. Moves beyond individual prompt crafting to designing entire autonomous systems. Era of 'Prompt Engineer' giving way to more sophisticated 'Agent Architect' roles. Salary range $91K-$284K; Google offering $153K-$222K base for similar GTM positions. Chief digital process designer focus.
pipeline_readiness_notes: Strong posting volume and multi-company adoption. Clear distinction from traditional architect roles due to agentic focus. Ready for enrichment. Recommend Job Zone 4-5 with emphasis on agentic orchestration frameworks and business process knowledge.

example_posting.title: LLM Fine-tuning Specialist
example_posting.snippet: We are hiring LLM Fine-tuning Specialists to optimize large language models for specific applications. You will curate and preprocess training data, design tuning methodologies, implement evaluation pipelines, and collaborate with teams to create repeatable model tuning workflows.

core_responsibilities: Fine-tune open-source LLMs using LoRA, RLHF, DPO, and model distillation
Curate and preprocess training data for model optimization
Design and implement model evaluation pipelines and benchmarking
Evaluate model performance against baselines
Collaborate with data and prompt teams on tuning workflows
Document methodologies and maintain model registries
typical_background: Machine learning or data science background
Experience with Python and deep learning frameworks
Knowledge of fine-tuning techniques (LoRA, RLHF, DPO, distillation)
Data preprocessing and evaluation skills
Understanding of model architectures and tokenization
Experience with popular LLM frameworks
notes: 53,570 LLM Fine Tuning jobs on Indeed.com. Average hourly pay $36.91 (range $19-$52.88/hr). Skills include LoRA, RLHF, DPO, model distillation, benchmark reporting. Top hiring companies include Odixcity Consulting, Invisible Agency, Designlab. Many positions remote. Rapidly professionalizing from freelance/gig work to full-time roles. Closely related to 'Training Data Manager' role.
pipeline_readiness_notes: Strong posting volume and established training methodologies. Clear career path from data roles to specialized fine-tuning. Ready for enrichment. Recommend Job Zone 3-4 based on experience level. Monitor for salary/compensation growth as role matures.

example_posting.title: AI Operations Manager
example_posting.snippet: We are seeking an AI Operations Manager to oversee the deployment, monitoring, and optimization of AI systems in production. This role requires coordination across data, engineering, and ML teams to ensure reliability, performance, and cost-efficiency of AI infrastructure.

core_responsibilities: Oversee deployment and monitoring of AI systems
Manage AI infrastructure costs and optimization
Coordinate between data, engineering, and ML teams
Establish metrics and SLAs for AI system performance
Troubleshoot production issues and escalations
Document operational procedures and runbooks
typical_background: Operations or program management background
Experience with infrastructure or platform operations
Understanding of ML/AI systems and metrics
Project management skills
Data analysis and metrics design
Cross-functional collaboration skills
notes: Newer role emerging as organizations scale AI operations. Often roles are titled differently (AI Ops, ML Operations Manager, AI Platform Manager). Early stage; varied job responsibilities across companies. Limited posting volume but growing adoption signal. May consolidate with MLOps or mature into distinct specialization.
pipeline_readiness_notes: Experimental status - posting count below 50 and role responsibilities vary across companies. Recommend monitoring for 2-3 months before enrichment. May emerge as standalone role or merge with MLOps/Platform Engineering roles.

example_posting.title: Prompt Architect
example_posting.snippet: We are seeking a Prompt Architect to design sophisticated prompt engineering strategies, create prompt templates for reuse, and develop prompt optimization frameworks that improve model performance across applications.

core_responsibilities: Design sophisticated prompt engineering strategies
Create and maintain prompt template libraries
Develop prompt optimization and evaluation frameworks
Test and iterate on prompts for specific tasks
Document best practices and share learnings
Collaborate with product and engineering teams
typical_background: Writing or technical writing background preferred
Experience with LLM APIs and prompt engineering
Understanding of model capabilities and limitations
Data analysis for prompt performance evaluation
Communication and documentation skills
Familiarity with RAG and context optimization
notes: Evolution from Prompt Engineer as role becomes more strategic and architectural. Job titles vary significantly (Prompt Engineer, Prompt Designer, Prompt Optimization Specialist). Lower posting count (52) suggests role is consolidating into broader categories or emerging slowly. May remain niche or expand with LLM adoption.
pipeline_readiness_notes: Experimental status - posting count and signal strength lower than emerging threshold. Title variation and consolidation with other roles suggest this may not be distinct occupation. Recommend monitoring quarterly; may evolve into context optimization or prompt design specialization.
